stories.title: Tunnel Sleeves
stories.content: I try on the hoodie. It drapes over me like a snuggie and hugs my legs. I stretch my arms until the tips of my fingers poke out of its tunnel sized sleeves. It’s a bit big. I put it back and scan the other pieces in the aisle. Everything is one size fits all except for me. Typical thrifting.

stories.title: Bookworm
stories.content: I open the book, fanning its pages between my fingers. Something falls out and lands in the spine of page 58. As it inches out, I bend down to get a closer look. It’s a bookworm. It tells me it's read this one before, but it wanted to reread it. I ask it to tell me its favorite parts and it ends up reciting the entire story from beginning to page 58, to end.

stories.title: Friday Plans
stories.content: It’s Friday! My monstera’s leaves point to the ceiling in excitement. I power my laptop down, push back my chair, and raise my arms to join it in its celebration. I fill its silent hoorays with a loud woohoo. My monstera has the same plans every weekend: drink water, photosynthesize, and sleep. Tonight, I want to watch a recently released movie. So I turn its pot towards the tv and together we do something new.

stories.title: The Coatrack
stories.content:
Tucked away in the corner of the entry way, it sat nearly empty.

 In the dead of winter, when the coats resurged from the closet, it would bear a heavier load. Parties, though they rarely occurred, brought in couture coats it blushed holding. Blizzards came with thick and flannel lined jackets that made it stronger. Each time the front door opened it'd eagerly gaze at the layers being worn and flex its branches to support them. But it only felt warmth from the household’s residents in the coldest of times. When the flowers bloomed, it'd lose its coats, like a dog shedding its fur. And once again, it’d go unnoticed.

In its off season, its expectations hung low. Until, one sunny afternoon, a tote bag was put on it. The next day, a bike helmet. A new visitor was frequenting the household. Quickly the new visitor became an acquaintance, a regular, and then a fixture of the household. With each day, as they came and went, something new was added to the coatrack's branches. A hula-hoop, a scarf, a phone charger, a keychain, and for a short time, a candy necklace, were some of the items it previously hung.

It grew bigger week over week and became a visual centerpiece of the entryway. At the end of each day, for the first time, it felt full. Now when the household awoke, whether it was warm or cold, the rack stretched its arms excitedly, making room for the day’s addition.

stories.title: Biggest Thumb
stories.content:
His thumb looked bigger. It was a little too long. He put his hands together to compare. It was longer, he was sure of it.

The next day, Peter measured again. His thumb nearly reached the knuckle of his index finger now. He immediately scheduled an appointment with his doctor.

"Ive seen this before", his doctor said while pulling and prodding at his thumb.

"Your thumb wants to be more like your other fingers. Just be glad your other fingers don’t want to be more like your thumb. You’d have quite the stubby hand. What a disaster that’d be".

How was this not a disaster, Peter panicked. This was a catastrophe. 12 unhelpful minutes later and $350 poorer, Peter was on his way home no closer to solving his thumb's sudden reach for new heights. With no other ideas to stop or stunt it, he let it grow.

Soon enough, Peter found advantages to his thumb’s new length. His grip strength was superb, he was unbeatable at thumb wars, and his pointing never went unnoticed. Eventually, Peter began to wonder when his other thumb would catch up in height. Of all his fingers, especially his biggest thumb, it felt a little too short.

stories.title: Bed Dough
stories.content:
Some nights I wish I slept on bread dough. It’d be my loaf of  bed. Its thick round edges would rise from the floor each morning as the sun peeked through the window. Slowly it’d grow until it met my night stand.

When dusk came, it’d be light and fluffy. With a slight dome at its center, I'd be ready to sink into it. After brushing my teeth and putting on pajamas, I’d take a running start and jump into bed. My body would spring back from its surface and then softly lower. It'd compress like pressed bubblewrap . I'd fall asleep instantly.

When I’d wake in the morning, I’d listen to the low hums and gurgles of the dough rising again. They’d be an alarm I’d never have to set. When I’d finally gain the strength to rise out of bed, I’d sit up and feel the dough stick to my back, trying to pull me back into a doze. And some days maybe I’d give in and spend all day in my load of bed.

stories.title: Girly Things
stories.content: They were out and about doing girly things. None of them had ever discussed what a girly thing was, but when one of them said it, they all nodded in unison. Tonight, they found themselves at Riley’s. It was a small bar in a small town, but for this group it was bigger. It was the place for girly things.
